```
AssertionError                                                                           
```

The above repeats for every block mined.

## Solution

Figure out why we hit this assertion and provide a fix.

Save and restart the node at the latest known valid state (#205)

- Save each state transition into the log prior to applying it.
  - Each transition must have an serial identifier.
  - This includes:
    - current mediated transfers states
    - all channels known balance proof (latest message, locks, and secrets)
- Save the a snapshot of resulting state after the state transition is applied.
  - The snapshot must have an accompanying identifier for the latest transition applied
- Recover the state of the node from the log files.

Background: Pretty much all linux distributions and their users are now (and for a long time already) denying direct root logins via ssh. "sudo" must be now used to execute commands that require an elevated session . This makes using SSHLibrary a bit difficult because the nice&easy "Execute Command" and "Start Command" can not be used for such sudo-requiring setup. The users of SSHLibrary are forced to the interactive prompt and the "raw"-commands like "read" and "write": first to enter elevated session via sudo ... and then all the actual commands need to be executed using the cumbersome interactive prompt as well.

Would it be possible to enhance the "Execute Command" and "Start Command" to execute the command in an elevated sudo session? (Maybe by passing sudo parameters for those KWs, or "Login" or "Open Connection" ...or something else).

issue_title: Look for places where 'pk' is hardcoded but detail_uri_name should be used instead
N+1 queries in dehydrate of m2m
issue_body:
Hi,

Awesome blowsome project :). Thanks for giving this much up for community.

But as every journey for everyone is not as smooth, so ...

We have a tastypie resource that depends on some other resource through m2m relationship.

Now we are doing some work in `save_m2m`, something like below

```
def save_m2m(self, bundle):
     super(ourResource, self).save_m2m(bundle)
     //some work
     return bundle
```

and after  that, before sending any data to client, it goes into dehydrate of m2m resource and under that, it have code something like 

```
self.m2m_resources = []
    m2m_dehydrated = []

    # convert the_m2ms to a QuerySet if it isn't already
    if type(the_m2ms) != QuerySet:
        the_m2ms = the_m2ms.all()

    # expects a QuerySet unlike super dehydrate method
    for m2m in the_m2ms:
        m2m_resource = self.get_related_resource(m2m)
        m2m_bundle = Bundle(obj=m2m, request=bundle.request)
        self.m2m_resources.append(m2m_resource)
        m2m_dehydrated.append(self.dehydrate_related(m2m_bundle, m2m_resource, for_list=for_list))
```

Now in it the last line `self.dehydrate_related` is causing n+1 queries since it executes each time the loop runs.

Also btw, we have already done `prefetch_related` on our querySet in Meta class. for related resource
Something like 

```
class Meta:
    detail_allowed_methods = ['patch']  
    list_allowed_methods = ['get', 'post']
    queryset = table.objects.using('default').select_related('table2') \
        .prefetch_related(
            Prefetch('table3',
                     queryset=table3.objects.select_related('table4', 'cols')
                     .prefetch_related('options', 'options__options')
                     .only(*FIELDS_TO_QUERY_API)
                     ),
            Prefetch('items',
                     queryset=Item.objects.select_related('item_data')
                     .only(*FIELDS_TO_QUERY_API)
                     ),
            'tableX',
            'tableX__options'
    )\
        .only(*FIELDS_TO_QUERY_PM_API)

    fields = (... our fields ...)
    serializer = UJsonSerializer()
    validation = ModelFormValidation(form_class=resourceForm)
    always_return_data = True
    ordering = ['started_at']
    filtering = {
        'status': ('in',)
    }
    cache = OnlyCacheHeaders()
```

And  currently n+1 queries happening when we do `post-list` in tastypie.

I am not sure how to debug and find root cause of issue.

I have an issue with the save_m2m method when using UUID as the resource ID. I get duplicate related records created even though the records already exist (as indicated by the fact they have resource_uris).

Sending a PATCH request to resource 'company' with the following data for 'industries':
```javascript
{
	"industries":[
		{"label":"a","resource_uri":"/api/v1/industry/31007933-864d-4046-9691-2cf5b45e0a66/"},
		{"label":"b","resource_uri":"/api/v1/industry/57c00d8f-030d-419a-8dbe-f6a93489e79f/"}
	]
}
```

This creates 2 more industries and adds them to industries relation rather than just adding the relation to the existing industry. 

I suspect this is another hard coding of PK instead of field specified in detail_uri_name.

Models:
```python
class Company(models.Model):
	uuid 					= models.UUIDField(default=uuid.uuid4)
	name 					= models.CharField(max_length=255)
	industries 				= models.ManyToManyField(Industry, related_name="company")

class Industry(models.Model):
	uuid 		= models.UUIDField(default=uuid.uuid4)
	label		= models.CharField(max_length=100)
```

Resources:
```python
class CompanyResource(ModelResource):
	industries = fields.ToManyField('api.tasty.IndustryResource', 'industries', null=True, full=True)

	class Meta:
		detail_uri_name='uuid'
		queryset = Company.objects.all()
		resource_name = 'company'
		always_return_data = True
		authentication = Authentication()
```

issue_title: Better exception handling for Tastypie API call
issue_body:
When there's an exception in Tastypie and it's not a 404, then when DEBUG is True, Tastypie will show a Django error page, and when DEBUG is False, Tastypie will display an error message in JSON format and call Django's `mail_admins()` to notify the admins about the error.

While this is very nice, some exceptions are not because of a bad/incomplete implementation of your API, but because the API call that was done was bad/incomplete.

For example, when I do a POST to my API with an unsuported serialization format by having a header like for example this:

    Content-Type: unknown/unknown;

Then Tastypie will raise a `UnsupportedFormat` exception. In development we get a nice Django error page which is nice for debugging. But in production, we get an email through `mail_admins()`. This means that if you have an API that's being implemented by a lot of people that make mistakes like these, you will be spammed with errors in your email, resulting in not being able to separate the real errors from the "non" errors.

I know you can overwrite the `wrap_view()` method and do your own error handling. However, I don't think this is the most beautiful way to do it. If you overwrite this method you have to do replicate a lot of logic from the original code.

In this case I actually prefer overwriting `_handle_500()` as I describe in this Stackoverflow answer:
http://stackoverflow.com/a/17678044/1248175

However, I think the best way to do fix this is the following:

Devide the Tastypie exceptions in two categories:
- Implementation exceptions
- API call exceptions

The implementation exceptions are raised because of a bad/incomplete Tastypie class implementation (like Resources).

The API call exceptions are raised because of a bad/incomplete API call.

Then the implementation exceptions WILL do `mail_admins()` and the API call exceptions WON'T. It would also be nice if the JSON error message would contain an explanation of why the error occured.

What are your thoughts?

issue_title: create_app factory is colliding with pytest-invenio
issue_body:
In the `pytest-invenio` documentation section for [E2E testing](https://pytest-invenio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#recommend-layout) there is a recommendation for a following import:
`from invenio_app.factory import create_app as create_ui_api`
In case of silly developers like me, that don't read the documentation and assume that if this code works:
```
from invenio_app.factory import create_ui

@pytest.fixture(scope='module')
def create_app():
    return create_ui
```
then this one will work as well:
```
from invenio_app.factory import create_app

@pytest.fixture(scope='module')
def create_app():
    return create_app
```
it will result in a surprise when they run tests (the error message can be different, but the problem is with trying to pass an argument, while the `create_app` function doesn't expect any):
```
        # Use create_app from the module if defined, otherwise use default
        # create_app fixture.
        create_app = getattr(request.module, 'create_app', create_app)
>       app_ = create_app(**app_config)
E       TypeError: create_app() got an unexpected keyword argument 'APP_DEFAULT_SECURE_HEADERS'

/Users/switowski/.virtualenvs/test-invenio-instance/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pytest_invenio/fixtures.py:218: TypeError
```
because the `create_app` from `invenio-app` collides with the `create_app` from `pytest-invenio`.


Wouldn't it be better to rename the `create_app` in invenio-app to `create_ui_api` (since it's basically it - a combination of UI and API)?

issue_title: makeotf: Within a ‘vkrn’ feature, value record format A should set YAdvance
issue_body:
The OpenType Feature File Specification says in [section 2.e.iv](http://www.adobe.com/devnet/opentype/afdko/topic_feature_file_syntax.html#2.e.iv) about Value Record Format A: “Here the ‹metric› represents an X advance adjustment, except when used in the ‘vkrn’ feature, in which case it represents a Y advance adjustment.”

However, with makeotf v2.0.90 of Nov 19 2015, the following feature file:
```
languagesystem DFLT dflt;
feature vkrn {
    position A -100;
} vkrn;
```

produces the following GPOS table when dumped with ttx:
```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ttFont sfntVersion="true" ttLibVersion="3.0">

  <GPOS>
    <Version value="1.0"/>
    <ScriptList>
      <!-- ScriptCount=1 -->
      <ScriptRecord index="0">
        <ScriptTag value="DFLT"/>
        <Script>
          <DefaultLangSys>
            <ReqFeatureIndex value="65535"/>
            <!-- FeatureCount=1 -->
            <FeatureIndex index="0" value="0"/>
          </DefaultLangSys>
          <!-- LangSysCount=0 -->
        </Script>
      </ScriptRecord>
    </ScriptList>
    <FeatureList>
      <!-- FeatureCount=1 -->
      <FeatureRecord index="0">
        <FeatureTag value="vkrn"/>
        <Feature>
          <!-- LookupCount=1 -->
          <LookupListIndex index="0" value="0"/>
        </Feature>
      </FeatureRecord>
    </FeatureList>
    <LookupList>
      <!-- LookupCount=1 -->
      <Lookup index="0">
        <!-- LookupType=1 -->
        <LookupFlag value="0"/>
        <!-- SubTableCount=1 -->
        <SinglePos index="0" Format="1">
          <Coverage>
            <Glyph value="A"/>
          </Coverage>
          <ValueFormat value="4"/>
          <Value XAdvance="-100"/>
        </SinglePos>
      </Lookup>
    </LookupList>
  </GPOS>

</ttFont>
```

Instead of `<Value XAdvance="-100"/>`, shouldn’t this be `<Value YAdvance="-100"/>` since the value record format A appears inside a `vkrn` feature?

issue_body:
Translation strings should not be composed programmatically. Instead, they should either be self-contained or constructed programmatically using ICU syntax.

see: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/1473#discussion_r116873811

While we're working on this, we should audit the messages themselves: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/1473#discussion_r116874856

we need to check in on what the right way to refer to user types is (e.g. using capitalization, single quotes, or just plain words)

issue_title: add another decimal point to import progress
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

With very large imports on very slow connections, it's sometimes hard to tell if progress is being made.

### Expected behavior

Instead of one decimal point, we should show two.

### User-facing consequences

Users might think that progress has totally stalled, even if it hasn't.

This was observed by @jamalex 

Also relevant because of work to auto-resume imports: #3510

issue_title: Decimal objects are not serialized correctly with implicit field creation
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce (I tested on version 1.2.6):

    from marshmallow import Schema
    from datetime import datetime
    from decimal import Decimal

    obj = {'int': 1, 'decimal': Decimal('1.23'), 'datetime': datetime.now()}

    class MySchema(Schema):
        class Meta:
            fields = ['int', 'decimal', 'datetime']
    
    json = MySchema()
    json.dump(obj).data

The resulted object does not properly serialize the decimal.

issue_title: Some change in the Docs required , for security reasons json can't pass arrray
issue_body:
Docs to serialise SQLAlchemy with multiple rows suggest the code bellow:
```python
users_schema = UserSchema(many=True)

@app.route('/api/users/')
def users():
    all_users = User.all()
    result = users_schema.dump(all_users)
    return jsonify(result.data)
    # OR
    # return user_schema.jsonify(all_users)
```

This code will actually give error because list objects can't be jsonified, (ref this)[https://github.com/pallets/flask/issues/673]

Instead the example should be:
```python
users_schema = UserSchema(many=True)

@app.route('/api/users/')
def users():
    all_users = User.all()
    result = users_schema.dump(all_users)
    return jsonify({'data':result.data})
    # OR
    # return user_schema.jsonify({'data':result.data})
```

issue_title: set_tags() can't handle byte-array auxiliary tags
issue_body:
This is originally from [biostars](https://www.biostars.org/p/236819/). If you try to `set_tags()` using a byte array then the following error is produced:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "./foo.py", line 9, in <module>
        read.set_tags(tags)
      File "pysam/libcalignedsegment.pyx", line 2113, in pysam.libcalignedsegment.AlignedSegment.set_tags (pysam/libcalignedsegment.c:23066)
      File "pysam/libcalignedsegment.pyx", line 295, in pysam.libcalignedsegment.packTags (pysam/libcalignedsegment.c:6217)
    ValueError: invalid value type 'B' (<type 'str'>)

I'm attaching a very very small BAM file (it and the index are in the zip file) that can produce this error with the following code:

    #!/usr/bin/env python
    import pysam

    bam = pysam.AlignmentFile("small.bam")
    for read in bam.fetch():
        tags = read.get_tags(with_value_type=True)
        read.set_tags(tags)

I've tried earlier version (back to 0.8.4) and none of these are able to handle this sort of situation.

[example.zip](https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/files/777372/example.zip)

issue_title: fromQualityString() and toQualityString() renamed in v0.8.4
issue_body:
These are changes I've found (based on https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/compare/v0.8.3...v0.8.4) that are currently undocumented in the release notes (https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/blob/master/doc/release.rst):

- `fromQualityString()` -> `qualitystring_to_array()`
- `toQualityString()` -> `qualities_to_qualitystring()`

There may be others, but it is only the change from `fromQualityString()` to `qualitystring_to_array()` that has bitten me (see https://github.com/PeteHaitch/methtuple/issues/100). It'd be good with future releases to have a complete list of methods that have been removed/renamed, ideally with backwards compatibility for at least one release cycle.

Thanks for all your hard work on pysam.

issue_title: indent=1 prevents saving files with a single number
issue_body:
When saving a number of a file with `indent=1`, nothing is written:
```
>>> rapidjson.dumps(100,indent=1)
'100'
```
however
```
with open("zzz","w") as fd:
    rapidjson.dump(100,fd,indent=1)
```
creates an *empty* file `zzz`.

Note that 
```
with open("zzz","w") as fd:
    rapidjson.dump(100,fd)
```
and
```
with open("zzz","w") as fd:
    rapidjson.dump([100],fd,indent=1)
```
work correctly as expected.

We should support late acknowledgement of tasks and a way to support extending the ack deadline:

> If a long timeout is set for a long-running task but the task is interrupted prior to completion, it won't be restarted for some time. At the other extreme, if a short timeout is set, a long-running task will be considered incomplete and re-started with another worker. Support for extending timeouts during task execution exists in PubSub, this library should have a way to perform this late_ack.

We should figure out what we want this to look like from a user perspective.

Tag names will be as follows:

- `{pkg}-0.x.y` for releases to the `google-cloud-{pkg}` PyPI package, e.g. `core-0.21.1`, `datastore-0.22.0`, etc.
- `0.x.y` for releases of the umbrella package (as we had already been doing)

----

- For [hand-rolled stable][2] builds (i.e. `gh-pages`), we'll have a custom script which install from the latest version of each package on PyPI, rather than from the source tree (this may be a chicken-and-egg problem on tag builds that would both push docs **and** push to PyPI)
- We ~~will be disabling~~ have disabled (as of 1:47pm Pacific, Dec. 5, 2016) [RTD stable][1] builds: we can't have local filesystem [deps][3] on `latest` builds and then have PyPI deps on `stable` builds (RTD [subprojects][4] may help)

It seems that the new `google-cloud-pubsub` library version 0.28 does not include any support for pulling and processing messages in batches. Several of our production systems rely on processing pubsub messages in batches in order to reduce the load on other systems (e.g. to lower the amount of queries done our databases). Is there any ETA on re-adding batch pulling to the library?

Hi,

My lab is using Google Storage as a large image server, and we cut out the images into lots of chunks to fetch. I wrote a multithreaded client that can grab the chunks, but when the chunks are small enough, it seems the HTTP/1.1 overhead starts to become a significant issue and download speed falls dramatically. This is a classic use case for HTTP/2. Is there a way to fetch files using HTTP/2 streams that I'm not aware of? 

I did some searching around and it seems that grpc might already support HTTP/2. https://www.slideshare.net/GuoJing8/http2-and-grpc. I am also investigating using hyper to create requests session objects ((https://hyper.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html#requests-integration) to pass into Client (https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/storage/client.html).

Thanks again for your help @dhermes and co. :)

Will
